title: Active-duty Everett firefighter dies from occupational cancer after 30 years of service, department says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Everett firefighter John Benoit, 63, died from occupational cancer after serving over 30 years in the department.
Benoit was a motorcycle enthusiast and enjoyed activities like traveling, gardening, and birdwatching, according to his obituary.
Benoit, survived by his wife and two children, was laid to rest with services at Our Lady of Grace Church in Chelsea.

title: 2 Dorchester women charged in alleged human trafficking scheme
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Dorchester women, Yuberkys Recci and Keysha Ortiz, arrested for alleged multi-state human trafficking operation targeting a homeless victim.
Investigators linked the suspects to the crime using social media, GPS data, and commercial sex ads with the victim's details.
Recci faces charges including kidnapping and drug offenses, while Ortiz faces firearm charges; both held without bail pending a hearing.

title: ‘Brilliant, unique soul’: Mother remembers Fall River teen who drowned in pond
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Tristan Major, 17, drowned on July 12 while swimming with friends at South Watuppa Pond, Fall River.
His mother, Kailyn Lord, described Tristan as a 'brilliant, unique soul' and seeks help for his funeral costs.
Police divers recovered his body after a two-hour search; no foul play is suspected in the drowning.

title: Jennifer Garner posts throwback video of karaoke night at Nantucket restaurant
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jennifer Garner shared a throwback video from September 2025 of karaoke at Gaslight in Nantucket.
The video featured Garner, her co-stars and local guide Bill Jamieson performing ABBA's 'Dancing Queen'.
Garner was filming 'The Five-Star Weekend' in Nantucket, highlighting her enjoyable experiences on the island.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the yellow skies across the region
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Canadian wildfire smoke drifts into the Boston region, creating a yellowish sky and limiting expected heat on Tuesday.
Meteorologists predict the smoky haze will remain through Wednesday, possibly bringing a smoky smell and cooler temperatures.
Additional plumes of smoke from Ontario may impact the area on Wednesday and Thursday, according to local meteorologists.

title: A very special traffic cone arrives in Boston, courtesy of our new friends in Glasgow
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A signed traffic cone from Glasgow arrived in Boston, symbolizing a budding sister city relationship.
The cone will travel around Boston to support mental health charities in both Scotland and Massachusetts.
Boston and Glasgow aim to formalize sister city status by next year, strengthening cultural and business ties.

title: 3 more MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud, falsifying track inspections
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three MBTA employees are charged with overtime fraud and falsifying Red Line track inspections at Cabot Yard.
Four former MBTA employees, previously indicted, now face additional conspiracy charges related to the misconduct.
The accused allegedly worked on personal vehicles during shifts and falsified timesheets to claim overtime.

title: The World Cup was great, but Boston still doesn’t need the Olympics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France emerged victorious in the last of seven World Cup games hosted in Foxborough, concluding Boston's role in the event.
Boston's World Cup involvement led to a positive reception among locals, ranking "Boston Stadium" highly among U.S. venues.
Hosting the World Cup cost Massachusetts taxpayers nearly $100 million, contrasting with a potential $4.7 billion Olympic bid.

title: Bill Belichick’s daughter-in-law wanted for arrest in North Carolina after traffic stop
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jen Belichick, Bill Belichick's daughter-in-law, faces arrest in North Carolina for missing a court appearance for a speeding citation.
Jennifer Schmitt Belichick was cited for driving 88 mph in a 70 mph zone in Brunswick County, North Carolina.
Bill Belichick, former Patriots head coach, currently leads UNC's football program with Steve Belichick as the defensive coordinator.

title: Karen Read, Turtleboy look to get witnesses’ defamation suit tossed
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Karen Read and Aidan Kearney motion to dismiss a defamation lawsuit from witnesses in Read's murder case.
Witnesses allege Read and Kearney defamed them regarding the 2022 death of Boston Police Officer John O'Keefe.
The defamation lawsuit is challenged under Massachusetts' anti-SLAPP law, citing protected petitioning conduct.
